hints: String indexing starts at 0. The first character is word[0]; the last is word[-1].
A slice looks like word[start:stop]. The stop position is not included in the result — so to get 5 characters starting at 0, you write word[0:5] or word[:5].
To get everything from position 5 to the end, leave the stop blank: word[5:]. To reverse a string, use a step of -1: word[::-1].
Positions 7 through 10 inclusive means the stop must be 11, because the stop is always exclusive. Try word[7:11].
